name: Virginia National Bank
description_short: Virginia National Bank is an FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business banking, deposit accounts, loans, and treasury management services with online and mobile banking platforms.
description_long:
Virginia National Bank (VNB) is a full-service FDIC-insured bank backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The institution operates under Virginia National Bankshares Corp., a publicly traded holding company with an active board of directors and investor relations program. The bank has demonstrated ongoing leadership and operational development, as evidenced by recent board appointments and announced leadership transitions.

VNB offers comprehensive banking services across personal and business segments. Personal banking includes deposit accounts, personal IRAs, online and mobile banking, debit cards, credit cards, Zelle integration, Apple Pay, safe deposit boxes, and e-statement services. Business banking provides business deposit accounts, business IRAs, business online and mobile platforms, debit and credit cards, safe deposit boxes, and QuickBooks integration. The bank also provides personal and commercial loans, with dedicated trust and estate services for wealth management and corporate filings.

VNB distinguishes itself through specialized treasury management services including receivables solutions (ACH debits, deposit online), payables solutions (ACH credits, card programs, payroll services, positive pay, wire transfers), and liquidity management (sweep services, zero balance accounts). The bank operates a dedicated client care center with cybersecurity alerts, 24-hour telephone banking, and wire transfer instruction services. VNB also invests in community through a Finance Career & Leadership Academy, suggesting commitment beyond transactional banking.

As a traditional regional bank, VNB serves customers seeking established banking infrastructure with both personal and sophisticated business solutions. However, the website lacks specific information about product terms, rates, fees, minimum balances, credit card rewards, or loan rates. No detail is provided regarding geographic service areas beyond the Virginia branding, competitive positioning, or customer service metrics. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal deposit accounts and savings accounts
Personal IRA savings and retirement accounts
Business deposit accounts and business IRAs
Treasury management with ACH debits and credits
pros: FDIC insurance backed by full U.S. Government faith and credit
Comprehensive personal banking including IRA savings and retirement accounts
Robust business banking platform with treasury management and ACH solutions
Mobile and online banking with card controls and fraud notifications
Trust and estate services with corporate filing support
24-hour telephone banking and dedicated client care center
Zelle integration and Apple Pay for convenient payment options
cons: Website provides no information on interest rates, fees, or minimum balance requirements
No details on credit card rewards programs or loan terms available
Limited geographic service area information; unclear if nationwide or regional-only
No competitive rate comparisons or customer satisfaction metrics disclosed
Lacks transparency on APR ranges, account opening promotions, or fee schedules
best_for: Business owners seeking comprehensive treasury management and payroll services
Customers in Virginia seeking a traditional bank with established infrastructure
Individuals needing trust, estate planning, and wealth management services
Companies requiring ACH, wire transfer, and receivables management solutions
diagnosis: Virginia National Bank is best suited for established businesses and individuals seeking comprehensive banking and treasury management services from a traditional, FDIC-insured institution. The primary caveat is the website's lack of specific product details—rates, fees, minimum balances, and terms are not disclosed, requiring direct contact with the bank for accurate comparison and decision-making.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
